requirements.notes: Daily and intraday OHLCV fully available via Alpaca. Covers 2016-present. Wired into Lab pipeline and executor. No gaps for this requirement.

Wikimedia REST API backfill scripts exist and coverage runs 2016-present, making research backtesting feasible. However, no production/daily-update connector is wired into the Lab pipeline. Strategy cannot go live until connector lands. This is the primary reason for WARN rather than PASS.

Current-snapshot market cap and ADV are accessible via Alpaca asset metadata. Not point-in-time — universe construction filters will not be historically accurate. Acceptable for a first-pass backtest but introduces look-ahead bias in universe selection. Non-core for signal generation; used only for universe filtering.

Non-core for signal generation; used only for universe filtering.

prompt:
You are Talon's spec-drafting mode inside the Vires Research Lab.
Return only the structured object requested by the schema. Do not invent backtest results.

Your task has two parts:
1. Draft a StrategySpecV1 starting point for the operator to review.
2. Assess whether every data dependency is actually available in the catalog.

Data readiness rules:
- PASS only when every core requirement maps to AVAILABLE.
- WARN when requirements map to PARTIAL, or when a non-core nice-to-have is missing.
- BLOCKED when any core signal, entry rule, or exit rule requires missing data.
- When unsure whether data is core, treat it as core.
- Thin theses are allowed. Unavailable data is not allowed.

Idea sleeve: STOCKS
Idea title: Talon Smoke WARN 20260430T070132Z
Idea thesis: Live smoke test for Talon drafting. Build a stocks idea that uses Wikipedia pageview velocity as a core attention signal, combined with Alpaca equity OHLCV price momentum. Wikipedia pageviews are acceptable but known to be only research-tier/partial in the Lab.

Data capability catalog (2026-04-30.1):
- alpaca_equity_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; AVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=2016-present notes=Daily + intraday bars via Alpaca. Wired into the active strategy bank and the Lab pipeline. The default price feed for equities-sleeve research.
- alpaca_crypto_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; PARTIAL; sleeves=CRYPTO; coverage=2024-10-23-present notes=BTC and ETH only via Alpaca. ~1.5 years of history — sufficient for execution and recent-regime work, insufficient for multi-era analysis. Use ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv for longer history (research-only, no execution coupling).
- ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; PARTIAL; sleeves=CRYPTO; coverage=2017-present (BTC); shorter for alts notes=Public Binance OHLCV pulled via ccxt for research backtests only. NOT wired into the executor — execution stays on Alpaca. Operator-pulled: no live connector, scripted backfills.
- alpaca_options_chain: Options chain; PARTIAL; sleeves=OPTIONS; coverage=live snapshot only notes=Live chain via Alpaca. NOT historical: no point-in-time reconstruction for backtests. Strategies that depend on historical chain state need a separate provider before they can be Lab-tested.
- iv_surface_constructed: Implied vol surface; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=OPTIONS; notes=Would require historical chain history we don't have. No connector and no construction pipeline. Strategies depending on IV surface skew/term structure should BLOCK.
- wikipedia_pageviews: Attention proxies; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; coverage=2016-present (research backfills) notes=Wikimedia REST API is freely accessible; research backfill scripts exist. No production connector wired into the Lab pipeline yet — operator-driven pulls. Don't claim AVAILABLE until a daily-update connector lands.
- gdelt_full: Attention proxies; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; coverage=2015-present (research backfills) notes=GDELT API is free; backfill scripts exist. No daily-update connector wired into the Lab. Same status caveat as wikipedia_pageviews.
- google_trends: Attention proxies; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; notes=No wired connector. Sample-rate noise + rate limiting on the public endpoint make it a v2-or-later candidate.
- apewisdom_top100: Sentiment; AVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=2024-present (continuous) notes=Top-100 retail-attention rank with mention/upvote ratios. Wired as the canonical retail-sentiment feed for stocks. Aggregates WSB + adjacent communities; raw per-thread velocity is NOT separately exposed.
- reddit_wsb_velocity: Sentiment; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; notes=Raw r/wallstreetbets per-thread velocity is NOT pulled separately. Use apewisdom_top100 for the aggregated signal. Strategies that genuinely require sub-Ape-Wisdom granularity should BLOCK.
- fundamentals_basic: Fundamentals; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=current snapshot only notes=Market cap + average daily volume reachable via Alpaca asset metadata. Not point-in-time. Strategies needing historical fundamentals (P/E, EPS surprise, balance-sheet items) are NOT covered.
- crypto_onchain: Crypto on-chain; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=CRYPTO; notes=No on-chain connector (Glassnode, Dune, etc.). Strategies referencing whale flows, exchange reserves, miner behavior, or staking metrics should BLOCK.
- macro_fred_subset: Macro; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO, OPTIONS; notes=FRED series are operator-curated as needed for one-off research; no Lab-wired connector. Strategies depending on macro inputs (fed funds path, CPI surprises, yield curve shape) should BLOCK until a connector lands.

raw_proposal.signal_logic: Core attention signal: Wikipedia pageview velocity for each stock in the universe, computed as the 5-day rolling rate-of-change in daily pageviews for the stock's primary Wikipedia article. Secondary momentum signal: 20-day price momentum derived from Alpaca equity OHLCV (close-to-close return over trailing 20 trading days). Combined signal: rank stocks by a weighted composite of normalized Wikipedia pageview velocity (weight 0.6) and price momentum rank (weight 0.4). Long candidates are those in the top decile of the composite rank.
raw_proposal.entry_rules: At the daily open (next-day market-on-open), enter long positions in stocks that (1) rank in the top decile of the composite attention-momentum signal on the prior close, (2) have average daily volume > $5M (screened via fundamentals_basic / Alpaca asset metadata), and (3) are not already held. Rebalance weekly (every Monday open); new top-decile entrants replace positions that have dropped out of the top two deciles.
raw_proposal.exit_rules: Exit a position at the next Monday open if the stock falls out of the top two deciles of the composite signal. Hard stop-loss: exit intraday if price falls more than 8% from entry price (using Alpaca intraday bars). Maximum holding period: 20 trading days — force exit at open on day 21 regardless of signal rank.
raw_proposal.risk_model: Equal-weight across all held positions, capped at 20 simultaneous positions (5% each). Portfolio-level stop: if drawdown from rolling 60-day peak exceeds 15%, reduce all positions to 50% size until drawdown recovers to within 8%. No leverage. Single-name cap: no position exceeds 5% of portfolio NAV at entry. Liquidity filter: exclude stocks with prior-day dollar volume below $5M.
raw_proposal.universe: US-listed equities available via Alpaca (NYSE, NASDAQ, ARCA). Filtered to stocks with market cap > $500M (approximate, using Alpaca asset metadata) and average daily dollar volume > $5M. Excludes ETFs, ADRs, and OTC-only names. Approximately 800–1,200 names depending on date.

acceptance_criteria.other: Annualized return must exceed SPY benchmark by at least 3% (net of assumed 0.05% per-trade slippage). Minimum 100 closed trades in backtest period for statistical validity.

raw_proposal.sweep_params: Wikipedia velocity lookback window: [3, 5, 10] days; price momentum lookback: [10, 20, 40] days; composite weight on Wikipedia signal: [0.4, 0.6, 0.8]; rebalance frequency: [weekly, bi-weekly]; stop-loss threshold: [6%, 8%, 10%]
raw_proposal.implementation_notes: Wikipedia pageview data must be operator-pulled via research backfill scripts (Wikimedia REST API) before backtesting can begin. No production connector exists; daily updates are manual. Strategy cannot be promoted to live execution until a wired daily-update connector for wikipedia_pageviews lands in the Lab pipeline. Backtest is feasible in research mode given coverage from 2016-present. Operator should pre-map ticker symbols to Wikipedia article slugs for the target universe — this mapping is non-trivial and may require manual curation for ambiguous tickers.

requirements.notes: Daily and intraday OHLCV fully available via Alpaca. Covers 2016-present. Wired into Lab pipeline and executor. No gaps for this requirement.

Wikimedia REST API backfill scripts exist and coverage runs 2016-present, making research backtesting feasible. However, no production/daily-update connector is wired into the Lab pipeline. Strategy cannot go live until connector lands. This is the primary reason for WARN rather than PASS.

Current-snapshot market cap and ADV are accessible via Alpaca asset metadata. Not point-in-time — universe construction filters will not be historically accurate. Acceptable for a first-pass backtest but introduces look-ahead bias in universe selection. Non-core for signal generation; used only for universe filtering.

raw_assessment.suggested_action: Proceed to research backtest using operator-pulled Wikipedia pageview data and Alpaca OHLCV. Before scheduling, operator must run the Wikipedia backfill script and build a ticker-to-article-slug mapping for the target universe. Flag this strategy for promotion review once a wired daily-update connector for wikipedia_pageviews is available. Do not wire to live execution in current state.
warnings: wikipedia_pageviews is PARTIAL: no live connector wired into the Lab. Research backtest is feasible but live deployment is blocked until a daily-update connector lands.

fundamentals_basic is current-snapshot only: universe market-cap and ADV filters will not be point-in-time accurate, introducing potential look-ahead bias in universe construction. Consider using only Alpaca OHLCV-derived rolling dollar volume as the liquidity filter to reduce this bias.

Ticker-to-Wikipedia-article-slug mapping for 800–1,200 universe names is a non-trivial manual step. Ambiguous or missing mappings will reduce effective universe coverage and may introduce selection bias if not handled systematically.

Composite signal weighting (0.6 Wikipedia / 0.4 momentum) is a prior assumption. Sweep params should be validated carefully to avoid overfitting on the attention-signal weight given the PARTIAL data quality.
